docs.title: DeepEval - The LLM Evaluation Framework
docs.text:
Org: confident-ai GitHub: https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval License: Apache 2.0 Stars: 15.4k Latest Release: v4.0.2 (May 13, 2026) Date: 2026-05-14

DeepEval is a simple-to-use, open-source LLM evaluation framework, for evaluating large-language model systems. It is similar to Pytest but specialized for unit testing LLM apps. DeepEval incorporates the latest research to run evals via metrics such as G-Eval, task completion, answer relevancy, hallucination, etc., which uses LLM-as-a-judge and other NLP models that run locally on your machine.

Whether you're building AI agents, RAG pipelines, or chatbots, implemented via LangChain or OpenAI, DeepEval has you covered. With it, you can easily determine the optimal models, prompts, and architecture to improve your AI quality, prevent prompt drifting, or even transition from OpenAI to Claude with confidence.

docs.title: Custom All-Purpose Metrics
docs.text:
• G-Eval — LLM-as-a-judge metric for evaluating on any custom criteria with human-like accuracy
  • DAG — Graph-based deterministic LLM-as-a-judge metric builder

docs.title: Agentic Metrics
docs.text:
• Task Completion — evaluate whether an agent accomplished its goal
  • Tool Correctness — check if the right tools were called with the right arguments
  • Goal Accuracy — measure how accurately the agent achieved the intended goal
  • Step Efficiency — evaluate whether the agent took unnecessary steps
  • Plan Adherence — check if the agent followed the expected plan
  • Plan Quality — evaluate the quality of the agent's plan
  • Tool Use — measure quality of tool usage
  • Argument Correctness — validate tool call arguments

docs.title: RAG Metrics
docs.text:
• Answer Relevancy — measure how relevant the RAG pipeline's output is to the input
  • Faithfulness — evaluate whether the RAG pipeline's output factually aligns with the retrieval context
  • Contextual Recall — measure how well the RAG pipeline's retrieval context aligns with the expected output
  • Contextual Precision — evaluate whether relevant nodes in the RAG pipeline's retrieval context are ranked higher
  • Contextual Relevancy — measure the overall relevance of the RAG pipeline's retrieval context to the input
  • RAGAS — average of answer relevancy, faithfulness, contextual precision, and contextual recall

docs.title: Multi-Turn Metrics
docs.text:
• Knowledge Retention — evaluate whether the chatbot retains factual information throughout a conversation
  • Conversation Completeness — measure whether the chatbot satisfies user needs throughout a conversation
  • Turn Relevancy — evaluate whether the chatbot generates consistently relevant responses
  • Turn Faithfulness — check if the chatbot's responses are factually grounded across turns
  • Role Adherence — evaluate whether the chatbot adheres to its assigned role

docs.title: MCP Metrics
docs.text:
• MCP Task Completion — evaluate how effectively an MCP-based agent accomplishes a task
  • MCP Use — measure how effectively an agent uses its available MCP servers
  • Multi-Turn MCP Use — evaluate MCP server usage across conversation turns

docs.title: Multimodal Metrics
docs.text:
• Text to Image — evaluate image generation quality
  • Image Editing — evaluate image editing quality
  • Image Coherence — measure how well images align with accompanying text
  • Image Helpfulness — evaluate how effectively images contribute to user comprehension
  • Image Reference — evaluate how accurately images are referred to by text

docs.title: Other Metrics
docs.text:
• Hallucination — check factually correct information against provided context
  • Summarization — evaluate factual correctness of summaries
  • Bias — detect gender, racial, or political bias
  • Toxicity — evaluate toxicity in LLM outputs
  • JSON Correctness — check output matches expected JSON schema
  • Prompt Alignment — measure whether output aligns with prompt instructions

docs.title: Additional Features
docs.text:
• Supports both end-to-end and component-level LLM evaluation
  • Build custom metrics integrated with DeepEval's ecosystem
  • Generate single and multi-turn synthetic datasets
  • Seamless CI/CD integration
  • Auto-optimize prompts based on evaluation results
  • Benchmark ANY LLM on popular benchmarks (MMLU, HellaSwag, DROP, BIG-Bench Hard, TruthfulQA, HumanEval, GSM8K) in under 10 lines

docs.title: Frameworks
docs.text:
• OpenAI (client wrapper)
  • OpenAI Agents (end-to-end)
  • LangChain (callback handler)
  • LangGraph (callback handler)
  • Pydantic AI (type-safe validation)
  • CrewAI (multi-agent systems)
  • Anthropic (client wrapper)
  • AWS AgentCore
  • LlamaIndex
  • Google ADK
  • Strands

docs.title: Platform + Ecosystem
docs.text: Confident AI — all-in-one platform integrating natively with DeepEval: manage datasets, trace LLM applications, run evaluations, monitor in production. Also serves as data persistence layer with MCP server for IDE-based workflows (Claude Code, Cursor).

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install -U deepeval
deepeval login
deepeval test run test_chatbot.py

Key concepts: LLMTestCase (input, actual_output, expected_output, retrieval_context), metrics with threshold scoring (0-1), assert_test() for pytest integration, evaluate() for notebook environments.

docs.title: Env Variables
docs.text: Auto-loads .env.local then .env from CWD at import time. Precedence: process env -> .env.local -> .env.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
• Integration with Confident AI ✅
  • G-Eval ✅
  • RAG metrics ✅
  • Conversational metrics ✅
  • Evaluation Dataset Creation ✅
  • Red-Teaming ✅
  • DAG custom metrics ⬜
  • Guardrails ⬜

docs.title: Tongyi DeepResearch
docs.text:
Org: Alibaba-NLP (Tongyi Lab) GitHub: https://github.com/Alibaba-NLP/DeepResearch License: Apache 2.0 Stars: 18.9k Latest Release: N/A (300 commits) Date: 2026-05-14 Paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.24701

Tongyi DeepResearch is an agentic large language model featuring 30.5 billion total parameters, with only 3.3 billion activated per token (30B-A3B, 128K context). Developed by Tongyi Lab (Alibaba), specifically designed for long-horizon, deep information-seeking tasks.

State-of-the-art performance across agentic search benchmarks: Humanity's Last Exam, BrowseComp, BrowseComp-ZH, WebWalkerQA, xbench-DeepSearch, FRAMES, SimpleQA.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Fully automated synthetic data generation pipeline: Highly scalable data synthesis pipeline, fully automatic, empowers agentic pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning.
  • Large-scale continual pre-training on agentic data: Diverse, high-quality agentic interaction data to extend model capabilities, maintain freshness, strengthen reasoning.
  • End-to-end reinforcement learning: Strictly on-policy RL based on customized Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework, with token-level policy gradients, leave-one-out advantage estimation, selective filtering of negative samples.
  • Agent Inference Paradigm Compatibility: Two inference paradigms — ReAct (core intrinsic abilities) and IterResearch-based "Heavy" mode (test-time scaling for maximum performance ceiling).

docs.title: Model
docs.text:
Model Size Context Tongyi-DeepResearch-30B-A3B 30B-A3B 128K

Available on HuggingFace, ModelScope, OpenRouter.

docs.title: Deep Research Agent Family
docs.text: Extensive research family including WebWalker (ACL 2025), WebDancer (NeurIPS 2025), WebSailor, WebShaper, WebWatcher, WebResearcher, ReSum, WebWeaver, WebSailor-V2, AgentFold, WebLeaper, BrowseConf, ParallelMuse, AgentFrontier, and more.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
• Python 3.10.0, Conda environment
  • Dependencies: Serper.dev (web search), Jina.ai (page reading), OpenAI-compatible API (summarization), Dashscope (file parsing), SandboxFusion (Python sandbox)
  • Two input formats: JSONL and JSON
  • ReAct or IterResearch inference via run_react_infer.sh
  • OpenRouter API for GPU-free inference

docs.title: Workshop on Builing Your Own Deep Research Agent + Technical Writer Multi-Agent System
docs.text:
A hands-on workshop presented at AI Engineering Conference Europe, building a multi-agent AI system with two MCP servers: a Deep Research Agent and a LinkedIn Writing Workflow.

YouTube Workshop: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mYSRn6PC1mc Slides: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RWdS5VQYjz7a9y7NzHhAnyhGtxi6e0vt/view Course: https://academy.towardsai.net/courses/agent-engineering

docs.title: What You'll Build
docs.text:
Deep Research Agent — An MCP server that runs deep research using Gemini with Google Search grounding and native YouTube video analysis:

user topic → [deep_research] × N → analyze_youtube_video (if URLs) → [deep_research gap-fill] → compile_research → research.md

LinkedIn Writing Workflow — An MCP server that generates LinkedIn posts with an evaluator-optimizer loop:

research.md + guideline → generate post → [review → edit] × N → post.md → generate image

Both servers expose tools, resources, and prompts via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), letting any MCP-compatible harness orchestrate the workflow.

docs.title: Three Ways to Use This Repo
1. Watch the Workshop (2 hours)
docs.text: Watch the 2-hour YouTube workshop and slides. You'll come away with a mental model of the full multi-agent system: tool-use agents, evaluator-optimizer loops, grounded search, structured LLM output, and MCP-server design.

docs.title: 2. Run the Finished Code (30 min)
docs.text:
Watch the system generate a research brief, draft a LinkedIn post through an evaluator-optimizer loop, and score itself with an LLM-as-judge.

git clone https://github.com/iusztinpaul/designing-real-world-ai-agents-workshop.git
cd designing-real-world-ai-agents-workshop
cp .env.example .env  # Add your GOOGLE_API_KEY
uv sync
make test-end-to-end

docs.title: 3. Implement it Yourself with Agentic Coding (2–4 hours)
docs.text:
Open implement_yourself/, a stripped-down skeleton prepared with 25 pre-groomed tickets and a custom /implement Claude Code skill that orchestrates SWE and Tester agents in a loop.

No cheating, by design. implement_yourself/ is a self-contained project. Open your harness (Claude Code, Cursor, …) directly in that folder so its working directory is scoped to the skeleton. The agents can't see the reference implementation in ../src/.

docs.title: Patterns and Concepts You'll Learn
docs.text:
• Tool-use agents — letting the LLM decide which tools to call and when
  • Evaluator-optimizer loop — generate, review, edit in cycles
  • Grounded search — Gemini with Google Search grounding for factual research
  • Structured LLM output — Pydantic schemas for type-safe model responses
  • MCP server design — registering tools, resources, and prompts with FastMCP
  • LLM-as-judge evaluation — automated quality scoring with Opik

docs.title: 1. Start with a seed
docs.text:
A short research brief with 2-3 questions and reference links:

# Research Topic: AI Agent Architecture — When Less Is More

## Key Questions
1. Why do single-agent architectures with smart tools outperform multi-agent systems?
2. What are the only legitimate reasons to adopt a multi-agent architecture?

## References
- Stop Overengineering: Workflows vs AI Agents Explained (YouTube)
- From 12 Agents to 1 (DecodingAI article)

docs.title: 2. Deep Research Agent produces research.md
docs.text: The agent runs multiple Gemini-grounded search queries and analyzes YouTube videos, then compiles everything into a structured research brief with sources.

docs.title: 3. Write a guideline
docs.text: A short brief describing the post angle, audience, and key points.

docs.title: 4. Writing Workflow refines the post
docs.text: The evaluator-optimizer loop generates a draft, then runs 3 rounds of review + edit.

docs.title: Real Output Example
docs.text:
LinkedIn-style post card generated by the system:

Phil Tovaloo AI Engineer | I ship AI products and teach you about the process.

We planned 12 AI agents and shipped 1. It worked better.

Sounds crazy, right? But it's a common story.

A client built an AI marketing chatbot. Their initial design had dozens of agents: orchestrator, validators, spam prevention. It failed.

A single agent with tools won. Tasks were tightly coupled. One brain maintained context. Tools were still specialized.

The simplest system that reliably solves the problem is always the best system.

Don't overengineer your AI agents. Build simple first.

docs.title: Harness Engineering
docs.text:
Harness engineering isn't just a new term for prompt engineering. It's where AI is heading.

Think of it this way: prompt engineering is what to ask. Context engineering is what to send the model. Harness engineering is how the whole thing operates — the environment around the model.

A harness includes tools, permissions, state, tests, logs, retries, checkpoints, guardrails, and evals.

Real companies are already doing this: - Anthropic's long-running agents externalize memory into artifacts - OpenAI built a 1M-line product with zero manual code using structured docs and agent-to-agent reviews - Stripe agents merge 1K+ PRs weekly within isolated environments - LangChain moved a coding agent from outside the top 30 to top 5 on Terminal Bench 2.0 by changing only the harness

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: Component Tool LLM API Google Gemini (via google-genai SDK) MCP Framework FastMCP Data Validation Pydantic Settings Pydantic Settings Observability Opik Image Generation Gemini Flash Image QA Ruff Package Manager uv

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Check Install Python 3.12+ python --version uv python install 3.12 or python.org uv 0.7+ uv --version curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh (docs) Google API Key — aistudio.google.com/apikey (required — all LLM calls use Gemini) Opik account — comet.com/site/products/opik (optional, for observability and evals)

docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/iusztinpaul/designing-real-world-ai-agents-workshop.git
cd designing-real-world-ai-agents-workshop
cp .env.example .env  # Add your GOOGLE_API_KEY (+ optional OPIK_API_KEY)
uv sync

docs.title: Running the Code
docs.text: Mode Best for MCP Servers (recommended) Interactive use with AI harness Skills Guided slash-command workflows Streamlit UI Visual end-to-end demo with live progress Scripts Verify setup, smoke tests

make eval-test         # LLM judge on test split
make eval-online       # Generate + judge posts on the fly

docs.title: Feynman - AI Research Agent

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The open source AI research agent. Tự động tìm kiếm papers, web, thực hiện research, tạo literature review, audit papers, và replicate experiments.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# macOS / Linux
curl -fsSL https://feynman.is/install | bash

# Windows (PowerShell)
irm https://feynman.is/install.ps1 | iex

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: Command What it does /deepresearch <topic> Multi-agent investigation with parallel researchers /lit <topic> Literature review with consensus, disagreements /review <artifact> Simulated peer review with severity /audit <item> Paper vs. codebase mismatch audit /replicate <paper> Replicate experiments on local/cloud GPUs /compare <topic> Source comparison matrix /draft <topic> Paper-style draft from findings /autoresearch <idea> Autonomous experiment loop /watch <topic> Recurring research watch

docs.title: Agents
docs.text:
• Researcher — gather evidence across papers, web, repos, docs
  • Reviewer — simulated peer review with severity-graded feedback
  • Writer — structured drafts from research notes
  • Verifier — inline citations, source URL verification

docs.title: Skills & Tools
docs.text:
• AlphaXiv — paper search, Q&A, code reading
  • Docker — isolated container execution
  • Web search — Gemini or Perplexity
  • Session search — indexed recall across prior research
  • Preview — browser and PDF export
  • Modal — serverless GPU compute
  • RunPod — persistent GPU pods with SSH

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: Built on Pi for agent runtime, alphaXiv for paper search and analysis. Every output is source-grounded — claims link to papers, docs, or repos with direct URLs.

docs.title: Source
docs.text:
• Wiki Source
  • GitHub

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GBrain gives AI agents a persistent brain. Built by the President and CEO of Y Combinator (Garry Tan) to run his actual AI agents. Production deployment: 17,888 pages, 4,383 people, 723 companies, 21 cron jobs running autonomously.

  • Org: garrytan
  • GitHub: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain
  • Stars: 15.3k
  • License: MIT
  • GitHub Evals: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain-evals

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Hybrid Search — Vector + keyword + RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion)
  • Self-Wiring Knowledge Graph — Typed entity links (attended, works_at, invested_in, founded, advises) with zero LLM calls
  • PGLite — In-browser Postgres, no server needed, database ready in 2 seconds
  • 34 Skills — Organized by a resolver that tells the agent which skill to read for any task
  • MCP Server — 30+ MCP tools via stdio, HTTP with OAuth 2.1 support
  • Minions — Durable Postgres-native job queue for deterministic background work
  • Durable Agents — Subagent runs survive crashes with replay from last committed turn
  • Skillify — Turn any fixed bug into a permanent skill with a 10-item audit
  • Storage Tiering — Keep bulk content (tweets, articles, transcripts) out of git
  • BrainBench — P@5 49.1%, R@5 97.9% on 240-page corpus

docs.title: The 34 Skills
docs.text:
• Always-on: signal-detector, brain-ops
  • Content Ingestion: ingest, idea-ingest, media-ingest, meeting-ingestion, voice-note-ingest, article-enrichment
  • Research & Synthesis: book-mirror, strategic-reading, concept-synthesis, perplexity-research, archive-crawler, academic-verify, brain-pdf
  • Brain Operations: enrich, query, maintain, citation-fixer, repo-architecture, publish, data-research
  • Operational: daily-task-manager, daily-task-prep, cron-scheduler, reports, cross-modal-review, webhook-transforms, testing, skill-creator, skillify, skillpack-check, smoke-test, minion-orchestrator
  • Identity & Setup: soul-audit, setup, migrate, briefing

docs.title: Getting Data In
docs.text:
• Recipes: Voice-to-Brain (Twilio), Email-to-Brain, X-to-Brain, Calendar-to-Brain, Meeting Sync, Public Tunnel, Credential Gateway
  • Migrate: Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Brain Repo (git) ←→ GBrain (Postgres + pgvector) ←→ AI Agent (34 skills)

The repo is the system of record. GBrain is the retrieval layer. Human always wins — edit any markdown file and gbrain sync picks up changes.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
On an agent platform (OpenClaw or Hermes Agent) or standalone:

git clone https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain.git && cd gbrain && bun install && bun link
gbrain init
gbrain import ~/notes/
gbrain query "what themes show up across my notes?"

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "gbrain": { "command": "gbrain", "args": ["serve"] }
  }
}

Accessed: 2026-05-12

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Hermes Agent is a self-improving AI agent built by Nous Research. It's the only agent with a built-in learning loop - creating skills from experience, improving them during use, searching past conversations, and building a deepening model of the user across sessions. It runs on a $5 VPS, cloud VMs, or serverless infrastructure with near-zero idle cost.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Org: NousResearch
  • Stars: 98k
  • License: MIT
  • Commits: 4,770+
  • Contributors: 496+

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Built-in learning loop with autonomous skill creation
  • Persistent memory with periodic nudges and FTS5 session search
  • Honcho dialectic user modeling for cross-session recall
  • Skills system that self-improves during use
  • 40+ built-in tools
  • Multiple terminal backends: local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, Modal
  • Messaging gateway: Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Email
  • Full TUI with multiline editing, slash-commands, conversation history
  • Cron scheduler for automated tasks
  • MCP integration for extended capabilities
  • Multi-model support: Nous Portal, OpenRouter (200+ models), NVIDIA NIM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Hugging Face

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Start chatting
hermes

# Configure model
hermes model          # Interactive model selection
hermes model openai/gpt-5.4

# Configure tools
hermes tools

# Start messaging gateway
hermes gateway setup
hermes gateway start

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text:
hermes                  # Interactive CLI
hermes model            # Choose LLM provider
hermes tools            # Configure tools
hermes config set       # Set config values
hermes gateway          # Start messaging gateway
hermes setup           # Full setup wizard
hermes update          # Update to latest
hermes doctor          # Diagnose issues

docs.title: Slash Commands (CLI & Messaging)
docs.text: Action Command New conversation /new or /reset Change model /model [provider:model] Set personality /personality [name] Retry/undo /retry, /undo Compress context /compress Check usage /usage, /insights [--days N] Browse skills /skills, /

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
• Terminal backends: local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, Modal
  • Messaging platforms: Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Email, Home Assistant

docs.title: Migration from OpenClaw
docs.text:
hermes claw migrate              # Interactive migration
hermes claw migrate --dry-run # Preview
hermes claw migrate --preset user-data

docs.text: Hermes creates skills autonomously after complex tasks. Skills: - Automatically persist knowledge - Self-improve during use - Compatible with agentskills.io open standard

  • Honcho for user modeling
  • MCP server for external tool integration
  • SQLite for persistent storage

docs.title: HF Viewer — Visualize Any Hugging Face Model
docs.text:
Org: Embedl AB Website: https://hfviewer.com/ Date: 2026-05-14 Chrome Extension: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/hugging-face-viewer/mmadlggmpkpiockpjfepaohcllbnakej

HF Viewer (hfviewer.com) is a free web tool that provides interactive architecture visualization for any Hugging Face model. Paste a Hugging Face URL or repo name and jump straight into a clear interactive model view.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Instant Visualization: Paste a Hugging Face URL or repo ID to open an interactive model architecture graph directly in the browser, no installation needed
  • Granularity Levels: Switch between overview and detailed traced blocks/paths
  • Model Family Comparison: Family pages (e.g. Gemma 4) let you compare multiple related models with synchronized interaction
  • URL Magic: Replace huggingface.co with hfviewer.com in any model URL to view it directly
  • Chrome Extension: View each model directly from Hugging Face pages
  • Embeddable: Press "Embed" to get code to embed visualizations in model cards
  • Interactive Blog: Graph-to-text and text-to-graph loop for technical ML articles

docs.title: Popular Models Supported
docs.text:
• Gemma 4 family (E2B, E4B, 26B-A4B, 31B)
  • DeepSeek V4 Pro
  • YOLOv8n pose
  • ViT base, CLIP
  • GPT-2, T5
  • Qwen 3.5/3.6 family
  • Parakeet TDT speech recognizer
  • And any other Hugging Face model

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
1. Go to https://hfviewer.com/
  2. Paste a Hugging Face URL or repo name (e.g., Qwen/Qwen3.5-4B)
  3. Explore the interactive graph with zoom and granularity controls
  4. Optionally embed visualizations in model cards or use the Chrome extension

docs.title: LFM2.5-Audio-1.5B
docs.text: Organization: Liquid AI Model: 1.5B parameters

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Liquid AI's updated end-to-end audio foundation model. End-to-end multimodal speech and text language model - does not require separate ASR and TTS components.

docs.title: Key Improvements
docs.text:
• Custom, LFM based audio detokenizer
  • llama.cpp compatible GGUFs for CPU inference
  • Better ASR and TTS performance
  • Low latency for real-time conversation

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The model consists of:

  • Pretrained LFM2.5 as multimodal backbone
  • FastConformer based audio encoder for continuous audio inputs
  • RQ-transformer generating discrete tokens
  • Lightweight audio detokenizer for audio output

docs.title: Interleaved Generation
docs.text:
• Real-time speech-to-speech conversational chatbot
  • Audio generation latency is key

docs.title: Sequential Generation
docs.text:
• Non-conversational tasks (ASR, TTS)
  • Allows model to switch generated modality on the fly

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• 1.5B parameters achieves capabilities on par with much larger models
  • Seamless conversational interaction
  • Low latency design
  • Multimodal speech + text

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Standalone open-source PDF parsing tool focused on fast and light parsing. Provides high-quality spatial text parsing with bounding boxes, without proprietary LLM features or cloud dependencies. Everything runs locally.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Org: run-llama
  • Stars: N/A (check GitHub)
  • License: Apache 2.0

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Fast Text Parsing — Spatial text parsing using PDF.js
  • Flexible OCR System — Built-in Tesseract.js (zero setup), or plug in HTTP OCR servers (EasyOCR, PaddleOCR)
  • Screenshot Generation — Generate high-quality page screenshots for LLM agents
  • Multiple Output Formats — JSON and Text
  • Bounding Boxes — Precise text positioning information
  • Standalone Binary — No cloud dependencies, runs entirely locally
  • Multi-platform — Linux, macOS (Intel/ARM), Windows
  • Multi-format Input — Auto-converts Word, Excel, PPT, images to PDF via LibreOffice/ImageMagick

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Global install via npm
npm i -g @llamaindex/liteparse

# Or via Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew tap run-llama/liteparse
brew install llamaindex-liteparse

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Basic parsing
lit parse document.pdf

# Parse with JSON output
lit parse document.pdf --format json -o output.json

# Generate screenshots
lit screenshot document.pdf -o ./screenshots

# Batch parsing
lit batch-parse ./input-directory ./output-directory

docs.title: Library Usage
docs.text:
import { LiteParse } from '@llamaindex/liteparse';

const parser = new LiteParse({ ocrEnabled: true });
const result = await parser.parse('document.pdf');
console.log(result.text);

docs.title: OCR Options
docs.text:
• Default: Tesseract.js (zero setup, works out of the box)
  • Optional: HTTP OCR servers via simple API specification
  • Supported: EasyOCR, PaddleOCR, or custom HTTP OCR services

docs.text:
Author: Sebastian Raschka, PhD Website: https://sebastianraschka.com/llm-architecture-gallery/ Last Updated: April 10, 2026

This page collects architecture figures and fact sheets from posts on Sebastian Raschka's blog, plus selected release posts or technical reports when a new architecture has not been covered there yet.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The LLM Architecture Gallery is a comprehensive collection of LLM architecture factsheets covering various models. It includes: - Architecture figures and fact sheets - Comparison tools to compare two architectures side by side - AA Intelligence Index scores - KV cache calculations

docs.title: Models Included
docs.text: The gallery covers 70+ LLM architectures including:

docs.title: Attention Mechanisms
docs.text:
• MHA (Multi-Head Attention): Classic full attention
  • GQA (Grouped-Query Attention): Shares KV heads across query groups
  • MLA (Multi-Latent Attention): DeepSeek's latent KV attention
  • SWA (Sliding-Window Attention): Local context attention
  • QK-Norm: Query/Key normalization for training stability
  • NoPE (No Positional Encoding): Removes positional embeddings in some layers

docs.title: MoE (Mixture of Experts)
docs.text:
• Sparse MoE: Routes to subset of experts per token
  • Hybrid MoE: Combines transformer with state space models
  • Gated Attention: Learned gating mechanism
  • Latent MoE: Latent space routing

docs.title: 6. PEFT
docs.text:
GitHub: https://github.com/huggingface/peft

  • Fine-tune with minimal compute
  • LoRA, adapters, prefix tuning
  • Best for cost-efficient training

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Traditional fine-tuning is impractical for LLMs (billions of params; 100s GB). Since this kind of compute isn't accessible to everyone, parameter-efficient finetuning (PEFT) came into existence.

LLM weights are matrices of numbers adjusted during finetuning. Most PEFT techniques involve finding a lower-rank adaptation of these matrices, a smaller-dimensional matrix that can still represent the information stored in the original.

docs.title: 1) LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)
docs.text:
• Add two low-rank trainable matrices, A and B, alongside weight matrices.
  • Instead of fine-tuning W, adjust the updates in these low-rank matrices.
  • Even for the largest of LLMs, LoRA matrices take up a few MBs of memory.

docs.title: 2) LoRA-FA (Frozen-A)
docs.text:
While LoRA significantly decreases the total trainable parameters, it requires substantial activation memory to update the low-rank weights.

LoRA-FA freezes matrix A and only updates matrix B.

docs.title: 3) VeRA (Vector-based Random Matrix Adaptation)
docs.text:
• In LoRA, low-rank matrices A and B are unique for each layer.
  • In VeRA, A and B are frozen, random, and shared across all layers.
  • Instead, it learns layer-specific scaling VECTORS (b and d) instead.

docs.title: 4) Delta-LoRA
docs.text:
• It tunes the matrix W as well, but not in the traditional way.
  • Here, the difference (or delta) between the product of matrices A and B in two consecutive training steps is added to W.

docs.title: 5) LoRA+
docs.text:
• In LoRA, both matrices A and B are updated with the same learning rate.
  • Authors of LoRA+ found that setting a higher learning rate for matrix B results in better convergence.

Accessed: 2026-05-12

docs.title: 72 Techniques to Optimize LLMs in Production
docs.text: Source: https://blog.dailydoseofds.com/p/72-techniques-to-optimize-llms-in

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Comprehensive guide explaining 72 techniques to optimize LLMs in production, organized into 9 categories addressing prefill compute, decode memory bandwidth, and serving costs.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Author: Avi Chawla
  • Publication: Daily Dose of Data Science
  • Date: April 17, 2026

docs.title: 1. Model Compression (8 techniques)
docs.text: INT4, INT8, FP8, GPTQ, AWQ, SmoothQuant, Distillation, Pruning

docs.title: 2. Attention and Architecture (8 techniques)
docs.text: FlashAttention, PagedAttention, GQA, MQA, MLA, Sliding Window, MoE, Early Exit

docs.title: 3. Decoding (6 techniques)
docs.text: Speculative Decoding, Medusa, EAGLE, Lookahead, Prompt Lookup, Constrained Decoding, Multi-token Prediction

docs.title: 4. KV Cache (7 techniques)
docs.text: Prefix Caching, KV Offload, KV Quantization, H2O, SnapKV, Attention Sinks, Chunked Prefill

docs.title: 5. Batching and Scheduling (6 techniques)
docs.text: Continuous Batching, Dynamic Batching, Prefill-Decode Disaggregation, SLO-aware, Spot GPU, Request Deduplication

docs.title: 6. Parallelism and Kernels (7 techniques)
docs.text: Tensor Parallellism, Pipeline Parallellism, Expert Parallellism, Sequence Parallellism, CUDA Graphs, Kernel Fusion, Torch Compile

docs.title: 7. Application Caching (3 techniques)
docs.text: Prompt Caching, Semantic Caching, Exact-match Caching

docs.title: 8. Input/Output Shaping (6 techniques)
docs.text: Prompt Compression, Context Pruning, System Prompt Optimization, Response Length Caps, Few-shot Pruning, Context Distillation

docs.title: 9. Routing and Cost (6 techniques)
docs.text: Model Routing, Model Cascading, Classifier Routing, Multi-provider Failover, QoS Tiers, Task-specific Fine-tuning

docs.title: Key Insights
docs.text:
1. No single optimization matters - stack techniques layered on each other
  2. Gap between naive and optimized deployment: 5-8x cost efficiency
  3. Work shifted from model compression to serving optimization

docs.title: MCP Toolbox for Databases
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/googleapis/mcp-toolbox
  • Author: Google (googleapis)
  • Stars: 15.2k
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • Language: Go
  • Fetched: May 14, 2026

MCP Toolbox for Databases is an open source MCP server that connects AI agents, IDEs, and applications directly to enterprise databases.

Dual purpose: 1. Ready-to-use MCP Server (Build-Time): Instant database access from Gemini CLI, Google Antigravity, Claude Code, Codex via prebuilt generic tools (list_tables, execute_sql, etc.) 2. Custom Tools Framework (Run-Time): Framework to build specialized, secure AI tools with structured queries, semantic search, and NL2SQL

Originally named "Gen AI Toolbox for Databases", renamed to align with MCP.

docs.title: Supported Databases
docs.text:
• Google Cloud: AlloyDB, BigQuery, Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server), Spanner, Firestore, Knowledge Catalog
  • Others: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, CockroachDB, ClickHouse, Couchbase, Neo4j, Snowflake, Trino

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Prebuilt generic tools for instant data exploration
  • Custom tools framework via tools.yaml (sources, tools, toolsets, prompts)
  • Connection pooling, integrated auth (IAM), OpenTelemetry
  • SDKs: Python, JS/TS, Go, Java
  • Framework integrations: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Genkit, ADK
  • Agent Skills generation (agentskills.io spec)
  • Toolbox UI for testing (--ui flag)
  • Dynamic config reloading

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Binary
curl -L -o toolbox https://storage.googleapis.com/mcp-toolbox-for-databases/v1.2.0/darwin/arm64/toolbox

# Docker
docker pull us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/database-toolbox/toolbox/toolbox:1.2.0

# Homebrew
brew install mcp-toolbox

# NPM
npx @toolbox-sdk/server --config tools.yaml

docs.title: Mirage: A Unified Virtual File System for AI Agents
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/strukto-ai/mirage
  • Author: strukto.ai
  • Stars: 2.2k
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • Fetched: May 14, 2026

Mirage is a Unified Virtual File System for AI Agents: a single tree that mounts services and data sources like S3, Google Drive, Slack, Gmail, and Redis side-by-side as one filesystem.

AI agents reach every backend with the same handful of Unix-like tools, and pipelines compose across services as naturally as on a local disk. It's a simulated environment, agents see one filesystem underneath. Any LLM that already knows bash can use Mirage out of the box, with zero new vocabulary.

const ws = new Workspace({
  '/data':   new RAMResource(),
  '/s3':     new S3Resource({ bucket: 'logs' }),
  '/slack':  new SlackResource({}),
  '/github': new GitHubResource({}),
})

await ws.execute('grep alert /slack/general/*.json | wc -l')
await ws.execute('cat /github/mirage/README.md')
await ws.execute('cp /s3/report.csv /data/local.csv')

docs.title: About
docs.text:
• One filesystem, every backend. Every service speaks the same filesystem semantics, so agents reason about one abstraction instead of N SDKs and M MCPs.
  • Multiple resources, one filesystem: RAM, Disk, Redis, S3/R2/OCI/Supabase/GCS, Gmail/GDrive/GDocs/GSheets/GSlides, GitHub/Linear/Notion/Trello, Slack/Discord/Telegram/Email, MongoDB, SSH, and more.
  • Familiar bash tools across every mount. Agents reuse Unix-like tools instead of learning a new API per service.
  • Portable workspaces: clone, snapshot, and version your environment.
  • Python and TypeScript SDKs for embedding in FastAPI, Express, browser apps.
  • Works with: OpenAI Agents SDK, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, Pydantic AI, CAMEL, OpenHands.
  • Lightweight CLI + daemon plugs into Claude Code and Codex.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: AI Agent and Application → Mirage Bash and VFS → Dispatcher & Cache → Infrastructure and Remote

docs.title: Python
docs.text: uv add mirage-ai

docs.title: TypeScript
docs.text:
npm install @struktoai/mirage-node      # Node.js
npm install @struktoai/mirage-browser   # browser/edge
npm install @struktoai/mirage-core      # runtime-agnostic

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
curl -fsSL https://strukto.ai/mirage/install.sh | sh
npm install -g @struktoai/mirage-cli

docs.title: Quickstart (Python)
docs.text:
from mirage import Workspace
from mirage.resource.gdocs import GDocsConfig, GDocsResource
from mirage.resource.ram import RAMResource
from mirage.resource.s3 import S3Config, S3Resource
from mirage.resource.slack import SlackConfig, SlackResource

ws = Workspace({
    "/data":  RAMResource(),
    "/s3":    S3Resource(S3Config(bucket="my-bucket")),
    "/slack": SlackResource(SlackConfig()),
    "/docs":  GDocsResource(GDocsConfig()),
})

await ws.execute("cp /s3/report.csv /data/report.csv")
await ws.execute("grep alert /s3/data/log.jsonl | wc -l")
ws.snapshot("demo.tar")

docs.title: Agent Frameworks
docs.text: Mirage integrates as a sandbox/tool layer: - OpenAI Agents SDK: MirageSandboxClient plugs a Workspace as sandbox - Vercel AI SDK: mirageTools(ws) exposes workspace as typed tool set - LangChain, Pydantic AI, CAMEL, OpenHands adapters available

docs.title: Cache
docs.text: Two-layer cache on every Workspace: - Index cache — listings and metadata (TTL-based) - File cache — object bytes - Pluggable backends: RAM (default, 512 MB) or Redis (shared across workers)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Open-source managed agents platform that turns coding agents into real teammates. Assign tasks via issues, track progress, and build reusable skills over time. Works with Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, OpenCode, Hermes, Gemini, Pi, and Cursor Agent.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:

  • Autonomous Execution — Full task lifecycle management with real-time progress streaming via WebSocket
  • Reusable Skills — Every solution becomes a reusable skill for the team
  • Unified Runtimes — One dashboard for all compute (local daemons and cloud runtimes)
  • Multi-Workspace — Organize work across teams with workspace-level isolation

CLI commands: multica setup, multica daemon start, multica issue create, multica update

docs.title: Obsidian
docs.text:
Website: https://obsidian.md/

Obsidian là một ứng dụng ghi chú và quản lý "second brain" dựa trên Markdown, giúp bạn xây dựng và tổ chức kho kiến thức cá nhân/dự án dưới dạng các tệp .md liên kết với nhau như một graph kiến thức.

docs.title: Obsidian dùng để làm gì?
docs.text:
• Ghi chú cá nhân, nhật ký, journal, daily notes cho công việc và cuộc sống
  • Xây "knowledge base"/second brain cho dev, researcher, người học (lưu tài liệu, ý tưởng, trích dẫn, tài liệu nghiên cứu, meeting notes…)
  • Quản lý dự án và công việc (project docs, roadmap, task list) nhờ hệ thống liên kết, tìm kiếm và plugin
  • Viết bài, tài liệu kỹ thuật, blog draft bằng Markdown, có hỗ trợ LaTeX và code blocks

docs.title: 1. Markdown editor & file .md
docs.text:
• Hỗ trợ đầy đủ cú pháp Markdown: heading, list, table, bold/italic, link, image… trên nền file .md thuần, lưu ngay trên ổ đĩa của bạn
  • Hỗ trợ LaTeX để viết công thức toán, và code blocks có syntax highlighting cho lập trình
  • Vì là file .md nên bạn có thể đem sang dùng với các app khác (VS Code, Git, static site generator…)

docs.title: 2. Vault và tổ chức dữ liệu
docs.text:
• Mỗi "vault" là một thư mục trên máy, bên trong là hệ thống folder + file .md; bạn có thể tách vault cho cá nhân, công việc, side project, nghiên cứu
  • Hỗ trợ nhiều vault song song, giúp tách biệt kiến thức giữa các mảng khác nhau
  • Có sẵn "Help Vault" chứa tài liệu hướng dẫn ngay trong app

docs.title: 3. Link hai chiều và Graph View
docs.text:
• Cho phép tạo liên kết nội bộ giữa các note, với dạng link hai chiều (backlink) để thấy note nào đang trỏ đến note nào
  • Graph View hiển thị toàn bộ note dưới dạng mạng lưới node–edge, giúp bạn visualize mối liên hệ, zoom, filter, tô màu cụm chủ đề

docs.title: 4. Tìm kiếm, tag, metadata
docs.text:
• Tìm kiếm toàn văn bản rất nhanh, hỗ trợ query nâng cao và filter theo nhiều tiêu chí
  • Dùng tag và YAML frontmatter để thêm metadata (status, topic, project…) cho note, giúp tổ chức và truy vấn linh hoạt hơn

docs.title: 5. Các tiện ích ghi chú nâng cao
docs.text:
• Outline: hiển thị mục lục theo heading của note để dễ navigation
  • Daily notes: tạo note mới theo ngày (YYYY-MM-DD) dùng cho journal, worklog, review
  • Random notes: mở ngẫu nhiên một note trong vault để gợi ý ý tưởng hoặc ôn lại kiến thức cũ

docs.title: 6. Plugin và mở rộng
docs.text:
• Có hệ plugin rất phong phú (core + community plugin) để thêm tính năng như: kanban, calendar, task management, dataview, sync, publish…
  • Cho phép tuỳ chỉnh giao diện, theme, hotkey, template để phù hợp workflow cá nhân của bạn

docs.title: 7. Offline, hiệu năng và quyền riêng tư
docs.text:
• Obsidian hoạt động offline, toàn bộ ghi chú nằm trên máy của bạn; tìm kiếm cũng thực hiện cục bộ nên rất nhanh
  • Vì dữ liệu là file .md trên file system, bạn dễ dàng backup bằng Git, sync bằng Dropbox/iCloud/… hoặc dùng dịch vụ sync riêng của Obsidian nếu muốn

docs.title: oLLM - Large-Context LLM Inference
docs.text: Organization: Mega4alik Stars: 2,365 License: MIT

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: oLLM is a lightweight Python library for large-context LLM inference, built on top of Huggingface Transformers and PyTorch. It enables running models like gpt-oss-20B, qwen3-next-80B, or Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on 100k context using ~$200 consumer GPU with 8GB VRAM. No quantization is used—only fp16/bf16 precision.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Large context: Supports up to 100k token context
  • Consumer GPU friendly: Runs on ~$200 GPU with 8GB VRAM
  • No quantization: Uses fp16/bf16 precision only
  • Multiple model support: gpt-oss-20B, qwen3-next-80B, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, and more

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install --no-build-isolation ollm

# From source
git clone https://github.com/Mega4alik/ollm.git
cd ollm
pip install --no-build-isolation -e .

# For Nvidia GPUs with cuda (optional, speeds up inference)
pip install kvikio-cu{cuda_version}  # e.g., kvikio-cu12

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• Python
  • GPU with 8GB+ VRAM
  • Huggingface Transformers
  • PyTorch

Source: GitHub Date: 2026-04-22

docs.title: Onyx - The Open Source AI Platform
docs.text: Organization: onyx-dot-app Stars: 27.4k License: MIT

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Onyx (formerly Danswer) is the application layer for LLMs - bringing a feature-rich interface that can be easily hosted by anyone. Onyx enables LLMs through advanced capabilities like RAG, web search, code execution, file creation, deep research and more.

Deploy with a single command: curl -fsSL https://onyx.app/install_onyx.sh | bash

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Agentic RAG: Get best in class search and answer quality based on hybrid index + AI Agents for information retrieval
  • Deep Research: Get in depth reports with a multi-step research flow. Top of leaderboard as of Feb 2026.
  • Custom Agents: Build AI Agents with unique instructions, knowledge, and actions.
  • Web Search: Browse the web to get up to date information. Supports Serper, Google PSE, Brave, SearXNG, and others. Comes with an in house web crawler and support for Firecrawl/Exa.
  • Artifacts: Generate documents, graphics, and other downloadable artifacts.
  • Actions & MCP: Let Onyx agents interact with external applications, comes with flexible Auth options.
  • Code Execution: Execute code in a sandbox to analyze data, render graphs, or modify files.
  • Voice Mode: Chat with Onyx via text-to-speech and speech-to-text.
  • Image Generation: Generate images based on user prompts.

docs.title: Supported LLM Providers
docs.text:
Onyx supports all major LLM providers:

  • Cloud: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini
  • Self-hosted: Ollama, LiteLLM, vLLM

docs.title: Onyx Lite
docs.text: The Lite mode can be thought of as a lightweight Chat UI. It requires less resources (under 1GB memory) and runs a less complex stack. It is great for users who want to test out Onyx quickly or for teams who are only interested in the Chat UI and Agents functionalities.

docs.title: Standard Onyx
docs.text:
The complete feature set recommended for serious users and larger teams. Additional components:

  • Vector + Keyword index for RAG.
  • Background containers to run job queues and workers for syncing knowledge from connectors.
  • AI model inference servers to run deep learning models used during indexing and inference.
  • Performance optimizations for large scale use via in memory cache (Redis) and blob store (MinIO).

docs.title: Enterprise Features
docs.text:
• Collaboration: Share chats and agents with other members of your organization.
  • Single Sign On: SSO via Google OAuth, OIDC, or SAML. Group syncing and user provisioning via SCIM.
  • Role Based Access Control: RBAC for sensitive resources like access to agents, actions, etc.
  • Analytics: Usage graphs broken down by teams, LLMs, or agents.
  • Query History: Audit usage to ensure safe adoption of AI in your organization.
  • Custom code: Run custom code to remove PII, reject sensitive queries, or to run custom analysis.
  • Whitelabeling: Customize the look and feel of Onyx with custom naming, icons, banners, and more.

docs.title: Connectors
docs.text:
Keep knowledge and access up to sync across 40+ connectors:

  • Google Drive
  • Confluence
  • Slack
  • Gmail
  • Salesforce
  • Microsoft Sharepoint
  • GitHub
  • Jira
  • And more...

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text:
There are two editions of Onyx:

  • Onyx Community Edition (CE) is available freely under the MIT license and covers all of the core features for Chat, RAG, Agents, and Actions.
  • Onyx Enterprise Edition (EE) includes extra features that are primarily useful for larger organizations.

Source: GitHub Date: 2026-04-22

docs.title: Open CoDesign - Open Source AI Design Tool
docs.text: Organization: OpenCoworkAI Repository: OpenCoworkAI/open-codesign

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Open CoDesign is an open-source desktop AI design tool that aims to be a local, subscription-free alternative to Claude Design, focused on generating and iterating on interactive UI prototypes and other design artifacts from natural language prompts.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
An Electron-based desktop app that lets you use your own model/API keys (BYOK) to generate interactive prototypes, slides, marketing pages, and similar design outputs from prompts.

Positioned as an open-source, local Claude Design replacement with no subscriptions or hosted quota limits.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-model support: Built-in support for Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenRouter, SiliconFlow, local Ollama, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
  • BYOK (bring your own key): You paste your own API keys; the app does not ship with a hosted backend or embedded provider account
  • One-click config import: Can read your existing Claude Code or Codex config (e.g. ~/.codex/config.toml, ~/.claude/settings.json) and import providers, models, and keys automatically
  • Local-first: Configuration is stored under ~/.config/open-codesign/config.toml and encrypted with Electron safeStorage, with an explicit claim that no credentials are uploaded
  • Outputs: Generates artifacts that render in a sandboxed iframe as either HTML or live React components, and supports export to HTML, PDF (via local Chrome), PPTX, ZIP, and Markdown on-device
  • Interaction: Supports inline comments on elements in the preview (model rewrites only that region), tunable sliders for parameters like color/spacing/font, and quick switching between the last several designs kept "alive" for instant preview

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
From the quickstart and docs, you can build and run from source via:

git clone https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-codesign.git
pnpm install
pnpm dev

This requires Node 22 LTS and pnpm 9.15+, and runs a typical Electron + web stack with a sandboxed preview iframe for rendered artifacts.

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:
brew install --cask opencoworkai/tap/open-codesign

Or download open-codesign--arm64.dmg / -x64.dmg from GitHub Releases.

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
scoop bucket add opencoworkai …
scoop install open-codesign

Or download -x64-setup.exe / -arm64-setup.exe.

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
AppImage open-codesign-*-x64.AppImage from Releases.

Note: Current installers are marked as unsigned in v0.1.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Install the app via package manager or direct download
  2. On first launch, add a provider:
  3. Import from Claude Code / Codex config, or
  4. Manually paste an API key (provider auto-detected from prefix), or
  5. Use keyless mode with an IP-allowlisted proxy or local Ollama
  6. Open the Hub, pick one of the built-in demo prompts or type your own
  7. The tool generates and renders an artifact inside a sandboxed iframe as HTML or React
  8. Iterate via inline comments, sliders, or switching between recent designs, then export to your desired format

docs.title: Relation to Other OpenCowork Projects
docs.text:
Open CoDesign sits alongside open-cowork, which is an open-source Claude Cowork–style multi-agent desktop app by the same org. Together they aim to replicate the Claude Code/Cowork/Design ecosystem locally, with CoDesign focused specifically on visual and interactive design generation rather than coding or agent workflows.

Source: GitHub Date: 2026-04-22

docs.title: Overview

docs.title: OpenTelemetry
docs.text: Website: https://opentelemetry.io/

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OpenTelemetry, also known as OTel, is a vendor-neutral open source Observability framework for instrumenting, generating, collecting, and exporting telemetry data such as traces, metrics, and logs.

As an industry-standard, OpenTelemetry is supported by more than 90 observability vendors, integrated by many libraries, services, and apps, and adopted by numerous end users.

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• Traces: Track requests as they flow through distributed systems
  • Metrics: Numerical measurements of system behavior
  • Logs: Timestamp records of events
  • Instrumentation: Add observability to applications automatically or manually

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• API: Specifications for instrumentation
  • SDK: Implementations of the API
  • Collectors: Middleware for processing telemetry
  • Exporters: Connectors to observability backends

docs.title: Related Links
docs.text:
• GitHub: https://github.com/open-telemetry

Source: OpenTelemetry Date: 2026-04-22

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OptiLLM is an OpenAI API-compatible optimizing inference proxy that implements 20+ state-of-the-art techniques to dramatically improve LLM accuracy and performance on reasoning tasks - without requiring any model training or fine-tuning.

  • Org: algorithmicsuperintelligence
  • GitHub: https://github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/optillm
  • Stars: 3.6k
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/optillm/
  • HuggingFace Space: https://huggingface.co/spaces/codelion/optillm

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Instant Improvements: 2-10x better accuracy on math, coding, and logical reasoning
  • Drop-in Replacement: Works with any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint
  • 20+ Optimization Techniques: From simple best-of-N to advanced MCTS and planning
  • Zero Training Required: Just proxy your existing API calls through OptiLLM
  • Production Ready: Used in production by companies and researchers worldwide
  • Multi-Provider: Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cerebras, and 100+ models via LiteLLM

docs.title: Implemented Techniques
docs.text:
• MARS (Multi-Agent Reasoning System) - Multi-agent reasoning with diverse temperature exploration
  • CePO (Cerebras Planning and Optimization) - Combines Best of N, CoT, Self-Reflection, Self-Improvement
  • CoT with Reflection - Chain-of-thought with thinking, reflection and output sections
  • PlanSearch - Search algorithm over candidate plans for problem-solving
  • ReRead (RE2) - Improves reasoning by processing queries twice
  • Self-Consistency - Advanced self-consistency method
  • Z3 Solver - Uses Z3 theorem prover for logical reasoning
  • R Algorithm - R algorithm for problem-solving
  • LEAP - Learns task-specific principles from few shot examples
  • Round Trip Optimization (RTO) - Optimizes responses through round-trip process
  • Best of N Sampling (BON) - Generates multiple responses and selects the best
  • Mixture of Agents (MOA) - Combines responses from multiple critiques
  • Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) - MCTS for decision-making in chat responses
  • PV Game - Prover-verifier game approach at inference time
  • Deep Confidence - Confidence-guided reasoning with multiple intensity levels
  • CoT Decoding - Chain-of-thought decoding without explicit prompting
  • Entropy Decoding - Adaptive sampling based on token uncertainty
  • AutoThink - Query complexity classification with steering vectors

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text:
• System Prompt Learning (SPL) - Model acquires program solving knowledge and strategies
  • Deep Think - Gemini-like Deep Think using inference time scaling
  • Long-Context CePO (LongCePO) - Planning and divide-and-conquer for infinite context
  • Majority Voting - Selects most frequent answer from k candidates
  • MCP Client - Model Context Protocol client for external tools
  • Router - Routes requests to different approaches based on prompt
  • Chain-of-Code (CoC) - CoT combined with code execution
  • Memory - Short term memory for unbounded context length
  • Privacy - Anonymize PII in requests
  • Read URLs - Fetches URL content and adds to context
  • Execute Code - Code interpreter for Python execution
  • JSON - Structured outputs with outlines library
  • GenSelect - Generative Solution Selection
  • Web Search - Google searches with Selenium
  • Deep Research - Test-Time Diffusion Deep Researcher

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install optillm
export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key-here"
optillm

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker pull ghcr.io/algorithmicsuperintelligence/optillm:latest
docker run -p 8000:8000 ghcr.io/algorithmicsuperintelligence/optillm:latest

docs.title: Proven Results
docs.text:
• MARS on Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite: +30.0 points on AIME 2025 (43.3→73.3)
  • CePO on Llama 3.3 70B: +18.6 points on Math-L5 (51.0→69.6)
  • AutoThink on DeepSeek-R1-1.5B: +9.34 points on GPQA-Diamond
  • MOA on GPT-4o-mini: Matches GPT-4 on Arena-Hard-Auto
  • PlanSearch on GPT-4o-mini: +20% pass@5 on LiveCodeBench

Accessed: 2026-05-12

docs.title: PageIndex: Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG
docs.text: Homepage: vectify.ai/pageindex Chat Platform: chat.pageindex.ai Developer: pageindex.ai/developer GitHub: github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex

docs.title: 📢 Updates
docs.text:
• Agentic Vectorless RAG — A simple agentic, vectorless RAG example with self-hosted PageIndex, using OpenAI Agents SDK
  • Scale PageIndex to Millions of Documents — PageIndex File System is a file-level tree layer that lets PageIndex reason over an entire corpus
  • PageIndex Chat — Human-like document analysis agent platform for professional long documents, available via MCP or API
  • PageIndex Framework — Deep dive into PageIndex: an agentic, in-context tree index that enables LLMs to perform reasoning-based, human-like retrieval over long documents

docs.title: Introduction to PageIndex
docs.text:
Are you frustrated with vector database retrieval accuracy for long professional documents? Traditional vector-based RAG relies on semantic similarity rather than true relevance. But similarity ≠ relevance — what we truly need in retrieval is relevance, and that requires reasoning.

PageIndex is a vectorless, reasoning-based RAG system that builds a hierarchical tree index from long documents and uses LLMs to reason over that index for agentic, context-aware retrieval.

It simulates how human experts navigate and extract knowledge from complex documents through tree search, enabling LLMs to think and reason their way to the most relevant document sections.

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text:
• No Vector DB: Uses document structure and LLM reasoning for retrieval, instead of vector similarity search
  • No Chunking: Documents are organized into natural sections, not artificial chunks
  • Human-like Retrieval: Simulates how human experts navigate complex documents
  • Better Explainability and Traceability: Retrieval is based on reasoning — traceable and interpretable, with page and section references

PageIndex achieved state-of-the-art 98.7% accuracy on FinanceBench, outperforming vector-based RAG solutions in professional document analysis.

docs.title: PageIndex Tree Structure
docs.text:
PageIndex transforms lengthy PDF documents into a semantic tree structure, similar to a "table of contents" but optimized for LLMs. Ideal for: financial reports, regulatory filings, academic textbooks, legal or technical manuals.

Example tree structure:

{
  "title": "Financial Stability",
  "node_id": "0006",
  "start_index": 21,
  "end_index": 22,
  "summary": "The Federal Reserve ...",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "title": "Monitoring Financial Vulnerabilities",
      "node_id": "0007",
      "start_index": 22,
      "end_index": 28,
      "summary": "The Federal Reserve's monitoring ..."
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: 1. Install dependencies
docs.text: pip install --upgrade -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 2. Set LLM API key
docs.text:
Create a .env file with your LLM API key (supports multiple LLMs via LiteLLM):

OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key_here

docs.title: 3. Generate PageIndex structure
docs.text:
python run_pageindex.py --pdf_path /path/to/your/document.pdf

Optional parameters: - --model: LLM model (default: gpt-4o-2024-11-20) - --toc-check-pages: Pages to check for table of contents (default: 20) - --max-pages-per-node: Max pages per node (default: 10) - --max-tokens-per-node: Max tokens per node (default: 20000)

docs.title: Markdown support
docs.text: python run_pageindex.py --md_path /path/to/your/document.md

docs.title: Agentic Vectorless RAG
docs.text:
For a simple, end-to-end agentic vectorless RAG example using PageIndex with OpenAI Agents SDK:

pip install openai-agents
python examples/agentic_vectorless_rag_demo.py

docs.title: Case Study: Finance QA Benchmark
docs.text: Mafin 2.5 is a reasoning-based RAG system for financial document analysis, powered by PageIndex. It achieved 98.7% accuracy on the FinanceBench benchmark, significantly outperforming traditional vector-based RAG systems.

docs.text:

  • Developer: pageindex.ai/developer (MCP, API docs)
  • Cookbooks: docs.pageindex.ai/cookbook
  • Tutorials: docs.pageindex.ai/tutorials

PageIndex Blog, Sep 2025.

docs.title: Personal AI Infrastructure (PAI) — v5.0.0
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/danielmiessler/Personal_AI_Infrastructure
  • Author: Daniel Miessler
  • License: MIT
  • Stars: Active project by creator of fabric
  • Fetched: May 14, 2026

PAI is a Life Operating System built on Claude Code. It captures who you are, what you care about, and where you're trying to go — and helps you get there using AI that knows you.

docs.title: Architecture (3 layers)
docs.text:
• PAI — the OS itself: Skills, memory, the Algorithm, Telos, identity files
  • Pulse — Life Dashboard at localhost:31337
  • The DA — Digital Assistant: voice and personality

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Humans first, tech second
  • Ideal State drives everything (current state → ideal state transition)
  • Text over opaque storage (Markdown, avoid SQLite/Postgres)
  • Context scaffolding > model quality
  • Filesystem as context, no RAG
  • Memory that compounds (3 tiers: WORK, KNOWLEDGE, LEARNING)
  • Self-improvement loop
  • Skills as deterministic units (code → CLI → workflows → SKILL.md)
  • Thinking skills library (first principles, council debates, red team, etc.)

docs.title: Key Features (v5.0.0 — Life Operating System)
docs.text:
• Pulse — unified daemon (port 31337): voice, hooks, observability, cron, Life Dashboard (22 routes), wiki API
  • The DA — Digital Assistant identity layer with PRINCIPAL_IDENTITY + DA_IDENTITY
  • Algorithm v6.3.0 — seven-phase loop: OBSERVE → THINK → PLAN → BUILD → EXECUTE → VERIFY → LEARN
  • ISA — Ideal State Artifact: 12 sections, 5 identities, owned by ISA skill
  • Containment zones — structural privacy with 12 security gates
  • Memory v7.6 — WORK, KNOWLEDGE, LEARNING, RELATIONSHIP, OBSERVABILITY, STATE
  • 45 skills, 171 workflows, 37 hooks

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Pipecat is an open-source Python framework for building real-time voice and multimodal conversational agents. Orchestrate audio and video, AI services, different transports, and conversation pipelines effortlessly.

  • Org: pipecat-ai
  • GitHub: https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat
  • Stars: 12.1k
  • License: BSD-2-Clause
  • Documentation: https://docs.pipecat.ai
  • Website: https://pipecat.ai

docs.title: What You Can Build
docs.text:
• Voice Assistants – natural, streaming conversations with AI
  • AI Companions – coaches, meeting assistants, characters
  • Multimodal Interfaces – voice, video, images, and more
  • Interactive Storytelling – creative tools with generative media
  • Business Agents – customer intake, support bots, guided flows
  • Complex Dialog Systems – design logic with structured conversations

docs.title: Why Pipecat
docs.text:
• Voice-first: Integrates speech recognition, text-to-speech, and conversation handling
  • Pluggable: Supports many AI services and tools
  • Composable Pipelines: Build complex behavior from modular components
  • Real-Time: Ultra-low latency interaction with different transports (e.g. WebSockets or WebRTC)

docs.title: Available Services
docs.text:
• Speech-to-Text: AssemblyAI, AWS, Azure, Cartesia, Deepgram, ElevenLabs, Google, Groq (Whisper), Mistral, NVIDIA, OpenAI (Whisper), Whisper, xAI, and more
  • LLMs: Anthropic, AWS, Azure, Cerebras, DeepSeek, Gemini, Grok, Groq, Mistral, Ollama, OpenAI, Perplexity, Qwen, Together AI, and more
  • Text-to-Speech: AWS, Azure, Cartesia, Deepgram, ElevenLabs, Google, Hume, Kokoro, LMNT, MiniMax, OpenAI, Piper, and more
  • Speech-to-Speech: AWS Nova Sonic, Gemini Multimodal Live, Grok Voice Agent, OpenAI Realtime, Ultravox
  • Transport: Daily (WebRTC), FastAPI Websocket, LiveKit (WebRTC), WebSocket Server, WhatsApp, Local
  • Video: HeyGen, LemonSlice, Tavus, Simli
  • Memory: mem0
  • Vision & Image: fal, Google Imagen, Moondream
  • Audio Processing: Silero VAD, Krisp Viva, Koala, ai-coustics, RNNoise
  • Analytics & Metrics: OpenTelemetry, Sentry

docs.title: Pipecat Ecosystem
docs.text:
• Pipecat Subagents – Multi-agent systems with distributed pipelines and shared message bus
  • Pipecat Flows – Structured conversations with complex state management
  • Pipecat CLI – Create, monitor, and deploy projects
  • Whisker – Real-time Pipecat debugger
  • Tail – Terminal dashboard for Pipecat
  • Voice UI Kit – Beautiful UI components for voice AI
  • Client SDKs: JavaScript, React, React Native, Swift, Kotlin, C++, ESP32

docs.title: Proxy-Pointer RAG: Achieving Vectorless Accuracy at Vector RAG Scale and Cost
docs.text: Author: VNG Type: Research Paper

docs.title: Tổng quan
docs.text: Paper giới thiệu Proxy-Pointer RAG - phương pháp mới để đạt độ chính xác như vector search mà không cần vector database, với chi phí và scale như sparse retrieval.

docs.title: Vấn đề với Vector RAG hiện tại
docs.text:
• **Embedding Mismatch": Query và document embedding có thể khác nhau về semantic space
  • **Index Drift": Document thay đổi nhưng index không cập nhật
  • **Scaling Cost": Vector search tốn kém khi scale lên hàng triệu documents
  • **Metadata Overhead": Cần lưu trữ cả embedding và metadata

docs.title: Giải pháp Proxy-Pointer RAG
docs.text:
Sử dụng pointer-based proxy thay vì embedding:

  1. Sparse Retrieval: Dùng BM25 hoặc SPLADE để retrieve candidate ban đầu
  2. **Proxy Classification": Dùng LLM để classify candidate có liên quan không
  3. Proxy Refinement: Cuối cùng dùng LLM để extract chính xác passage cần thiết

docs.title: Kết quả
docs.text:
• Đạt vectorless accuracy - chính xác như dense vector search
  • Giữ được vector RAG scale - scale được như sparse retrieval
  • Giảm cost đáng kể so với vector search truyền thốngi

docs.title: Ứng dụng
docs.text:
• RAG systems cần scale lớn
  • Hệ thống cần giảm chi phí infrastructure
  • Nơi mà embedding quality không ổn định

docs.title: Qwen3-TTS
docs.text:
🤗 [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/collections/Qwen/qwen3-tts)   |   🤖 [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/collections/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS)   |   📑 [Blog](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3tts-0115)   |   📑 [Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15621)   🖥️ [Hugging Face Demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS)   |    🖥️ [ModelScope Demo](https://modelscope.cn/studios/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS)   |   💬 [WeChat](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen/blob/main/assets/wechat.png)   |   🫨 [Discord](https://discord.gg/CV4E9rpNSD)   |   📑 [API](https://help.aliyun.com/zh/model-studio/qwen-tts-realtime)

We release **Qwen3-TTS**, a series of powerful speech generation capabilities developed by Qwen, offering comprehensive support for voice clone, voice design, ultra-high-quality human-like speech generation, and natural language-based voice control. ## News - 2026.1.22: Released Qwen3-TTS series (0.6B/1.7B) based on Qwen3-TTS-Tokenizer-12Hz. ## Overview ### Introduction

Qwen3-TTS covers 10 major languages (Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, German, French, Russian, Portuguese, Spanish, and Italian) with multiple dialectal voice profiles. Key features: - **Powerful Speech Representation**: Qwen3-TTS-Tokenizer-12Hz achieves efficient acoustic compression and high-dimensional semantic modeling - **Universal End-to-End Architecture**: Discrete multi-codebook LM architecture for full-information end-to-end speech modeling - **Extreme Low-Latency Streaming Generation**: Dual-Track hybrid streaming, latency as low as 97ms - **Intelligent Text Understanding and Voice Control**: Natural language instruction-driven speech generation ### Model Architecture

### Released Models | Model | Features | Languages | Streaming | Instruction Control | |---|---|---|---|---| | Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-VoiceDesign | Voice design from descriptions | 10 languages | ✅ | ✅ | | Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice | Style control via instructions; 9 premium timbres | 10 languages | ✅ | ✅ | | Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base | 3-second rapid voice clone; fine-tuning base | 10 languages | ✅ | | | Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-CustomVoice | 9 premium timbres | 10 languages | ✅ | | | Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base | 3-second rapid voice clone; fine-tuning base | 10 languages | ✅ | | ## Quickstart 

pip install -U qwen-tts
### Python Usage 
import torch
import soundfile as sf
from qwen_tts import Qwen3TTSModel

model = Qwen3TTSModel.from_pretrained(
    "Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice",
    device_map="cuda:0",
    dtype=torch.bfloat16,
    attn_implementation="flash_attention_2",
)

wavs, sr = model.generate_custom_voice(
    text="Hello world",
    language="English",
    speaker="Ryan",
)
sf.write("output.wav", wavs[0], sr)
### Supported Speakers | Speaker | Description | Native Language | |---|---|---| | Vivian | Bright, slightly edgy young female voice | Chinese | | Serena | Warm, gentle young female voice | Chinese | | Uncle_Fu | Seasoned male voice with low, mellow timbre | Chinese | | Dylan | Youthful Beijing male voice | Chinese (Beijing Dialect) | | Eric | Lively Chengdu male voice | Chinese (Sichuan Dialect) | | Ryan | Dynamic male voice with strong rhythmic drive | English | | Aiden | Sunny American male voice with clear midrange | English | | Ono_Anna | Playful Japanese female voice | Japanese | | Sohee | Warm Korean female voice with rich emotion | Korean | ## Web UI Demo 
qwen-tts-demo Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice --ip 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
## vLLM Usage vLLM-Omni provides day-0 support for Qwen3-TTS (offline inference only currently). ## Fine Tuning Refer to [finetuning/](finetuning/) for detailed instructions. ## Evaluation Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base achieves SOTA results on: - Seed-TTS test set: WER 0.77 (zh), 1.24 (en) - Cross-lingual benchmarks: significantly outperforms CosyVoice2 - InstructTTSEval: Best voice design scores (APS 85.2 ZH, 82.4 EN) ## Links - **GitHub**: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3-TTS - **Hugging Face**: https://huggingface.co/collections/Qwen/qwen3-tts - **Paper**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.15621 - **Blog**: https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3tts-0115

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Personal AI assistant — easy to install, deploy locally or in the cloud, connect across channels, extend with skills. Formerly known as CoPaw. Built by AgentScope team.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Org: agentscope-ai
  • Stars: N/A (check GitHub)
  • License: Apache 2.0

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Local Deployment — Memory and data fully under your control; no third-party hosting
  • Skills Extension — Built-in scheduling, PDF/Office processing, news digest; custom skills auto-loaded
  • Multi-Agent Collaboration — Create multiple independent agents with inter-agent communication
  • Multi-Layer Security — Tool guard, file access control, skill security scanning
  • Multi-Channel — DingTalk, Feishu, WeChat, Discord, Telegram, and more
  • Local Models — Supports llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio (no API key needed)
  • Mission Mode — Autonomous multi-phase task execution
  • ACP Protocol — External agent delegation

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Social media: Daily hot post digests (Xiaohongshu, Zhihu, Reddit), video summaries
  • Productivity: Email & newsletter highlights, calendar organization
  • Creative: Describe goal → auto-execute → wake up to prototype
  • Research: Track tech & AI news, knowledge base search
  • Desktop: Organize and search local files, read & summarize documents

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Option 1: pip install
pip install qwenpaw
qwenpaw init --defaults
qwenpaw app

# Option 2: Script install (macOS/Linux)
curl -fsSL https://qwenpaw.agentscope.io/install.sh | bash

# Option 3: Docker
docker pull agentscope/qwenpaw:latest
docker run -p 127.0.0.1:8088:8088 \
  -v qwenpaw-data:/app/working \
  agentscope/qwenpaw:latest

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8088/ for the Console.

docs.title: Security Features
docs.text:
• Tool guard — Intercepts dangerous shell commands
  • File access guard — Restricts access to sensitive paths
  • Skill security scanning — Detects prompt injection, command injection, hardcoded keys
  • Local deployment — All data stored locally
  • Web Authentication — Optional login protection

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text:
qwenpaw init --defaults     # Initialize with defaults
qwenpaw app             # Start web UI
qwenpaw doctor           # Diagnostic command

docs.title: Rowboat — Open-Source AI Coworker with Memory
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/rowboatlabs/rowboat
  • Author: Rowboat Labs (Y Combinator S24)
  • Stars: 14.1k
  • License: Apache-2.0
  • Fetched: May 14, 2026

Rowboat is an open-source AI coworker that connects to your email and meeting notes, builds a long-lived knowledge graph, and uses that context to help you get work done — privately, on your machine.

Capabilities: - Build me a deck about our next quarter roadmap → generates PDF from knowledge graph - Prep me for my meeting with Alex → pulls past decisions, open questions, relevant threads - Track a person, company or topic through live notes - Visualize, edit, and update knowledge graph anytime (plain Markdown) - Record voice memos that auto-capture key takeaways

docs.title: How It's Different
docs.text: Most AI tools reconstruct context on demand. Rowboat maintains long-lived knowledge instead: - Context accumulates over time - Relationships are explicit and inspectable - Notes are editable by you (not hidden in a model) - Everything lives on your machine as plain Markdown - Obsidian-compatible vault with backlinks

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
• Gmail (email), Google Calendar, Rowboat meeting notes / Fireflies
  • Composio.dev for product integrations
  • MCP for external tools (Exa search, Twitter/X, ElevenLabs, Slack, Linear/Jira, GitHub)

• Local models via Ollama or LM Studio

  • Swap anytime, data stays in local Markdown vault

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Meeting prep from prior decisions, threads, open questions
  • Email drafting grounded in history and commitments
  • Docs & decks generated from ongoing context (PDF slides)
  • Follow-ups: capture decisions, action items, owners
  • Live notes: auto-updating notes (@rowboat) that track competitors, topics, people

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Scrapling is an adaptive Web Scraping framework that handles everything from a single request to a full-scale crawl. Its parser learns from website changes and automatically relocates elements when pages update. The fetchers bypass anti-bot systems like Cloudflare Turnstile out of the box, and its spider framework scales to concurrent, multi-session crawls with pause/resume and automatic proxy rotation.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Org: D4Vinci
  • Stars: 37.7k
  • Forks: 3.3k
  • License: BSD-3-Clause
  • Commits: 1,400+
  • Language: Python 99.9%

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Adaptive element tracking that relocates elements after website changes using intelligent similarity algorithms
  • Scrapy-like Spider API with async parse callbacks, concurrent crawling, and per-domain throttling
  • Multiple fetcher types: Fetcher (HTTP), StealthyFetcher (anti-bot bypass), DynamicFetcher (browser automation)
  • Multi-session support with persistent cookies and state across requests
  • Built-in proxy rotation with cyclic or custom strategies
  • MCP server for AI-assisted web scraping and data extraction
  • CLI with interactive shell and extract command for terminal-based scraping
  • Pause & resume with checkpoint-based crawl persistence
  • Streaming mode for real-time stats and UI integration
  • Full type hints and 92% test coverage

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
from scrapling.fetchers import Fetcher, StealthyFetcher
from scrapling.spiders import Spider

# Basic HTTP request
page = Fetcher.get('https://quotes.toscrape.com/')
quotes = page.css('.quote .text::text').getall()

# Stealthy fetching (bypasses Cloudflare)
page = StealthyFetcher.fetch('https://example.com', headless=True, solve_cloudflare=True)
products = page.css('.product', auto_save=True)

# Full spider with concurrent crawling
class QuotesSpider(Spider):
    name = "quotes"
    start_urls = ["https://quotes.toscrape.com/"]
    concurrent_requests = 10

    async def parse(self, response):
        for quote in response.css('.quote'):
            yield {"text": quote.css('.text::text').get()}

result = QuotesSpider().start()

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install scrapling                    # Parser only
pip install "scrapling[fetchers]"     # With fetchers
pip install "scrapling[all]"        # Everything including CLI
scrapling install                   # Download browsers

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text:
scrapling shell                    # Interactive scraping shell
scrapling extract get 'https://example.com' content.md
scrapling extract stealthy-fetch 'https://example.com' output.html --solve-cloudflare

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• 10x faster JSON serialization than standard library
  • 784x faster than BeautifulSoup
  • 41x faster than Selectolax

docs.text:

  • Playwright - Browser automation used by DynamicFetcher
  • Scrapy - Similar Python scraping framework

docs.title: Segmentation with Internet-Augmented Multimodal LLMs
docs.text: Type: Research Paper

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Các mô hình segmentation dựa trên multimodal LLM như LISA, SESAME, READ có thể làm "reasoning segmentation" nhờ kiến thức có sẵn trong LLM.

docs.title: Vấn đề
docs.text:
LLM có knowledge cutoff cố định và việc huấn luyện lại rất tốn kém. Chúng thường không biết các thực thể mới xuất hiện sau cutoff: - iPhone 17 Pro Max (ra 2025) - "Tổng thống Mỹ hiện tại"

Điều này dẫn đến thất bại khi phải segment các thực thể: - Novel entities - thực thể mới - Emerging entities - thực thể đang diễn biến, cần thông tin cập nhật từ thế giới thực

docs.title: Giải pháp
docs.text: Dùng Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) để cho mô hình truy cập web thời gian thực trong lúc inference, từ đó khắc phục hạn chế về kiến thức.

docs.title: Related Concepts
docs.text: • Classification by combining to Internet RAG

docs.text:
• PDF
  • arXiv

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: SkillClaw makes LLM agents progressively better by evolving reusable skills from real session data. Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
• Org: AMAP-ML
  • Stars: 741
  • Forks: 78
  • License: MIT
  • Language: Python 98.9%, Shell 1.1%
  • Paper: arXiv:2604.08377

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Collective Skill Evolution - Skills evolve from every session, across agents, devices, and users
  • Auto-evolve, auto-deduplicate, auto-improve - Makes learned content actually count
  • Single user + multiple agents - One unified skill library across all agents
  • Multiple devices - Skills follow you, not your machine
  • Team sharing - Join shared groups, experience compounds
  • Hermes integration - Native integration with Hermes agent
  • Broad compatibility - Works with Hermes, OpenClaw, QwenPaw, IronClaw, PicoClaw, ZeroClaw, NanoClaw, NemoClaw

docs.title: 1. Client Proxy
docs.text: Local API proxy that intercepts agent requests, records session artifacts, and manages local skill library.

docs.title: 2. Evolve Server (Optional)
docs.text: Service that reads session data, evolves/creates skills, and writes back. Two engines: - workflow: Fixed 3-stage LLM pipeline (Summarize → Aggregate → Execute) - agent: OpenClaw-driven agent workspace with direct skill editing

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
git clone https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw.git && cd SkillClaw
bash scripts/install_skillclaw.sh
source .venv/bin/activate

# Setup
skillclaw setup

# Start
skillclaw start --daemon

docs.title: Hermes Integration
docs.text:
# If using Hermes
skillclaw setup  # choose hermes for CLI agent
skillclaw start --daemon

# Verify
hermes chat -Q -m skillclaw-model -q "Reply with exactly HERMES_SKILLCLAW_OK"

docs.title: Storage Support
docs.text:
• Local filesystem
  • Alibaba OSS
  • AWS S3

docs.title: Deployment Models
docs.text:
